title: Synthesis of New Bis(Indolyl)Methanes Catalyzed by Benzylsulfamic Acid and Evaluation of Their Antimicrobial Activities
author: M Heravi; Majid
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-2.0
description: Benzylsulfamic acid has been prepared and introduced as a new heterogeneous acid catalyst. This reagent was used for the synthesis of new bis(indolyl)methanes 3a – 3f via reaction of bis-aldehydes 1 with indoles 2 at 100°C. All reactions were performed under solvent-free conditions with high to excellent yields. The synthesized bis(indolyl)methanes were evaluated for their antibacterial and antifungal activities.
steps_raw: Benzylsulfamic acid has been prepared and introduced as a new heterogeneous acid catalyst. All reactions were performed under solvent-free conditions with high to excellent yields. The synthesized bis(indolyl)methanes were evaluated for their antibacterial and antifungal activities.
